records.ety: [OF. preie, F. proie, L. praeda, probably for praeheda. See Prehensile, and cf. Depredate, Predatory.]

defs: That which is or may be seized by animals or birds to be devoured; hence, a person given up as a victim.
quotes: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey.
Already sees herself the monster's prey.

defs: The act of devouring other creatures; ravage.
quotes: Hog in sloth, fox in stealth, . . . lion in prey.

records.ety: [OF. preier, preer, L. praedari, fr. praeda. See Prey, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Preyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Preying.]

defs: To take booty; to gather spoil; to ravage; to take food by violence.
quotes: More pity that the eagle should be mewed, While kites and buzzards prey at liberty.
